file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.thinking:
The job YAML looks correct already - it has the right image (apache/spark:3.5.3), serviceAccountName (spark-pi), and memory format (512Mi). The RBAC role is missing "pods" from the resources list.

The job is currently running. Let me wait for it to complete or fail, and check logs. But the known issues say there are 4 issues to fix. Looking at the current YAML:

1. Image: `apache/spark:3.5.3` - looks correct (not nonexistent)
2. serviceAccountName: `spark-pi` - looks correct
3. Memory: `512Mi` - also correct
4. RBAC: missing "pods" resource - this is definitely a real issue

The YAML file seems to already have some fixes applied, but the RBAC is still broken. Let me wait to see if the running job completes or fails, then address the RBAC issue since that's clearly preventing proper pod access.

I should check the pod logs to see what's actually happening.

content.text: The YAML file looks already corrected. Let me check the running pod's logs and fix the RBAC issue (missing "pods" resource).

input.description: Add pods to RBAC role resources

content.text: Now let me poll for the job to complete:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].type}' 2>/dev/null)
  SUCCEEDED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) condition=$STATUS succeeded=$SUCCEEDED failed=$FAILED"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Complete" ] || [ "$SUCCEEDED" = "1" ]; then
    echo "Job completed successfully!"
    break
  fi
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ] || [ "$FAILED" = "1" ]; then
    echo "Job failed!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll job status until complete or failed

description: Poll job status until complete or failed
